node.name: Infimum
node.statement: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $i\in S$. The element $i$ is the infimum of $A$ if and only if $i$ is a lower bound of $A$ and every lower bound $l$ of $A$ satisfies $l\le i$.

current_dependencies.name: Least Upper Bound Property
current_dependencies.gloss: [Least Upper Bound Property] Every nonempty subset bounded above has a supremum.

current_dependencies.name: Lower Bound
current_dependencies.gloss: [Lower Bound] l is a lower bound of A iff every x in A satisfies l <= x.

layering.detail: definition's closure touches non-definitional node(s) via def:least-upper-bound-property -> def:supremum -> ...
